comments.body: "I now need to figure out if a user clicked on the Window but outside the JDialog." ..why? See What is the XY problem?
Thanks! Yep, I actually totally forgot why I even need to catch that click ^^
Voting to close as a problem that can no longer be reproduced; if not, please update the question.
Please explain the close request? I still have the problem... or is this a request for more example code?
I interpreted your comment to mean that you no longer "need to catch that click." If you do need to respond to a click, why not use a modeless dialog?
The widow that opened the JDialog is supposed to be blocked -> modal does that just fine. But IF someone clicks outside the Modal Dialog, it is supposed to close.

I don't this your users understand the concept of a blocking dialog. Why make it blocking if you can just click on the window to close the dialog? You are not describing your problem effectively. I also agree this is an XY problem.

answers.body:
If your application window needs to receive events despite the dialog box is open, just make sure it is not modal.

Modal windows are designed to take focus and input from other windows to force the user first respond to that dialog before doing something else. If that is not what you intend to do, just do not use that feature. Dialog windows can be opened without the modal flag as well.

But be aware code that uses modal dialogs looks simple, while code that opens nonmodal windows will have to receive the user input in an event based manner and is thus more complex.

title: Catch MouseEvent on window blocked by modal dialog?
body:
I have a JFrame, which opens a modal JDialog. The JFrame has quite a few buttons, which are not supposed to be usable, when the JDialog is open -> therefore, modal JDialog.

However, when the area outside the JDialog is clicked, the JDialog is supposed to close. The solution I outline below doesn't catch any MouseEvents on the JFrame, though.

I now need to figure out if a user clicked on the window, but outside the JDialog. Reason to do this as per comment: The JDialog is supposed to close once the user clicks outside it!

My best guess currently is to create an AWTEventListener (As in how to obtain mouse click coordinates outside my window in Java):

listener = new AWTEventListener() {
    @Override
    public void eventDispatched(AWTEvent event) {
        if (!event.getSource().getClass().equals(ButtonDialog.class)
                && event.getID() == 502) {
            ButtonDialog.this.dispose();
        }
    }
};

and then add it when the JDialog opened:

Toolkit.getDefaultToolkit().addAWTEventListener(
    listener, AWTEvent.MOUSE_EVENT_MASK);

Unfortunately, when the dialog is modal, no AWT-events seem to be happening outside that dialog.

I have considered using the GlassPane of the JFrame instead, but that really seems impractical. Can I catch events on a "modally blocked" window like this at all? Or am I on the totally wrong track?
